description: A turn where the model asks the user a structured question (ask_user_question interrogative) → the user answers → message_complete.

request_body.content: I'm torn between two approaches for a small refactor. Use your ask_user_question tool to ask which I prefer, with exactly two short options (A: extract a helper; B: inline it). Keep the options terse. After I answer, do NOT implement anything — reply with just one short sentence acknowledging my choice, then stop.

questions.question: Extract a helper or inline the logic?
questions.context: You mentioned a small refactor with two approaches on the table. This is a narrow, low-stakes design choice — pick whichever fits better with how you see the surrounding code evolving.

options.label: A — Extract a helper
options.description: Pull the logic into a named helper function.

options.label: B — Inline it
options.description: Keep the logic inline at the call site.
